claims.summary: Projection epoch writes must not collide, leave stale temp files, or clobber a valid existing epoch during atomic swap failure.
claims.rationale: The codec projection suite verifies runtime temp naming, no-clobber install, valid concurrent epoch preservation, missing-candidate restore behavior, corrupt epoch replacement, and stale cleanup.

claims.summary: Projection metadata should remain deterministic across restarts and OS/runtime-dir differences.
claims.rationale: Projection runtime-dir and kernel-epoch tests verify env precedence, executable-relative fallback, stable fingerprints, and fresh-runtime-dir stability.

claims.summary: Tail vibrancy, semantic attenuation, and adaptive gain need explicit visibility so smoothstep does not hide clipping or false intensity.
claims.rationale: Existing CODEC_MAP, tail-vibrancy, effective attenuation, and vibrancy-ceiling transparency tests expose the bounded behavior while keeping live delivery unchanged.
